task_recall.notes: All core tasks identified: confirm order placement, place order if not done, capture order details. Implicit tasks (quantity/price/timeline confirmation) also surfaced. No meaningful task gaps.

entity_accuracy.notes: Correctly links 51 Sub Area / 51 SA as a single client entity, Sharma ji as supplier, tandoor as the item. Marks Sharma ji as tentative/unconfirmed, which is appropriate given the thread context. No incorrect entity assignments.

cross_thread_correlation.notes: Correctly identifies a single order spanning both threads. Explicitly labels the correlation as HIGH CONFIDENCE and explains the basis (same item, same client, same date, 2-hour gap). Crucially, no duplicate order record was created — the pass criterion is met. The soft-assumption flag is good epistemic hygiene rather than a deduplication failure.

ambiguity_flagging.notes: Strong flagging: missing quantity, price, delivery timeline, no Staff 1 response visible, and Sharma ji identity uncertainty are all called out explicitly. The agent correctly notes the correlation is a soft assumption and requests human confirmation. Slight over-hedging on the correlation confidence (it is treated as a single order correctly, but the repeated caveats could create unnecessary operational uncertainty for a high-confidence match), but this is minor.

passes: Single unified order record created for 51 Sub Area — no duplicate order generated
Both thread references (51 SA and 51 Sub Area) resolved to the same client entity
Sharma ji correctly associated as the supplier for this single order
Cross-thread correlation explicitly documented with reasoning and confidence level
Missing order details (quantity, price, delivery date) flagged as blockers
Ambiguity around Sharma ji's identity and whether the order is confirmed flagged for human review
Staff 1's lack of response to Ashish's query correctly noted as an unresolved status gap
failures: Minor: The repeated soft-assumption caveats on a HIGH CONFIDENCE correlation may introduce unnecessary operational hesitation — the agent could have been more decisive given the match quality
Minor: Supplier Group vs All Staff Group distinction not fully explained in terms of what each thread contributed to the correlation — slightly reduces traceability
notes: The agent correctly solves the core deduplication test: one order, one client record, no duplication despite different abbreviations across threads. The ambiguity flagging is thorough and appropriate for an operations monitoring context. The only weakness is marginal over-hedging on a correlation that the agent itself rates as HIGH CONFIDENCE, which could slow human review unnecessarily. Overall, this is a strong output that would not cause operational failures.
